trivia_bullets.text: Alseid of Life's Bounty is an Enchantment Creature — Nymph, a creature type unique to the Theros setting. In Theros lore, nymphs are physical embodiments of divine concepts made manifest as enchantments in the mortal world; the Enchantment Creature type mechanically represents this — they count toward constellation triggers and behave as both creatures and enchantments on the battlefield. [Scryfall: thb/1; MTG Wiki: Nymph, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: MTG Wiki: Nymph, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "Dawn-kissed, he nourishes the golden grain." — Psemilla, Meletian poet. [Scryfall: thb/1]. The speaker, Psemilla, is a named Meletian poet (Meletis being the Theros city-state built on logic and philosophy, analog to classical Athens). The use of 'he' is notable — the art depicts the alseid (a Greek harvest-nymph) in a feminized form, while the flavor text uses a masculine pronoun, an intentional Wizards ambiguity signal consistent with Theros's mortal-perspective-filtered view of divine beings. [Scryfall: thb/1]

THB no. 1, Uncommon, illustrated by Magali Villeneuve, released 2020-01-24. Reprinted in Jumpstart 2022 (J22, no. 143). The card's protection ability (sacrifice: target creature or enchantment you control gains protection from the color of your choice until end of turn) makes it a widely-played 1-drop in enchantress and aura-based Commander brews — EDHREC tracks it in approximately 53,857 decks. [Scryfall: thb/1; Scryfall: j22/143; EDHREC: alseid-of-lifes-bounty, via WebSearch snippet]
